# VibeCody — AI-Powered Developer Toolchain

> VibeCody is an open-source monorepo (Rust + React/TypeScript) providing VibeCLI (terminal AI assistant) and VibeUI (desktop code editor). MIT License.

## Plugin Development Quick Start

Create a plugin:
```bash
vibecli --plugin create my-plugin --kind connector
```

Plugin directory layout:
```
~/.vibecli/plugins/my-plugin/
  plugin.toml       # Manifest (required)
  skills/           # Markdown skill files
  hooks/            # Shell scripts for events
  commands/         # REPL commands
```

## Plugin Manifest (plugin.toml)

```toml
name = "my-plugin"
version = "1.0.0"
display_name = "My Plugin"
description = "Description"
author = "Author"
license = "MIT"
kind = "connector"  # connector | adapter | optimizer | theme | skillpack | workflow | extension

capabilities = ["FileRead", "NetworkAccess"]
# Capabilities: FileRead, FileWrite, NetworkAccess, ProcessExec, EnvRead, Notification, Clipboard, GitAccess, DatabaseAccess, HttpServer, WebSocket

[[hooks]]
event = "PostToolUse"      # SessionStart | UserPromptSubmit | PreToolUse | PostToolUse | Stop | TaskCompleted | SubagentStart | FileSaved | FileCreated | FileDeleted
tools = ["write_file"]     # Tool name filter
handler = "hooks/my-hook.sh"
priority = 100
async_exec = false

[[commands]]
name = "my-cmd"
description = "What it does"
handler = "commands/my-cmd.sh"

[[settings]]
key = "api_url"
description = "API endpoint"
type = "string"            # string | number | boolean | secret | filepath | enum
required = true
```

## Skills (.md with YAML front-matter)

```markdown
---
triggers: ["keyword1", "keyword2"]
tools_allowed: ["read_file", "write_file", "bash"]
category: "my-category"
---

# Skill Content

Instructions injected into the agent's system prompt when triggers match.
```

Skill locations (all loaded):
1. `<workspace>/.vibecli/skills/` (project-specific)
2. `~/.vibecli/skills/` (user-global)
3. `~/.vibecli/plugins/*/skills/` (from plugins)

## Hooks

Shell hooks receive JSON event on stdin. Exit 0 = allow, exit 2 = block (stderr = reason).

```bash
#!/bin/bash
EVENT=$(cat)
# Process event JSON
exit 0
```

Configuration in ~/.vibecli/config.toml:
```toml
[[hooks]]
event = "PreToolUse"
tools = ["bash"]
handler = { command = "python3 .vibecli/hooks/check.py" }

[[hooks]]
event = "FileSaved"
paths = ["**/*.rs"]
handler = { url = "https://example.com/webhook", method = "POST", timeout_ms = 5000 }
async = true
```

## AI Provider Trait (Rust)

```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait AIProvider: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    async fn is_available(&self) -> bool;
    async fn chat(&self, messages: &[Message], context: Option<String>) -> Result<String>;
    async fn stream_chat(&self, messages: &[Message]) -> Result<CompletionStream>;
    async fn complete(&self, context: &CodeContext) -> Result<CompletionResponse>;
    fn supports_vision(&self) -> bool;
}
```

17 providers: Ollama, Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Grok, Groq, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, Bedrock, Copilot, Mistral, Cerebras, DeepSeek, Zhipu, Vercel AI, LocalEdit, Failover.

## Tool System

XML-based tool calling (works with all providers):

```xml
<tool_call><name>read_file</name><path>src/main.rs</path></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>write_file</name><path>out.txt</path><content>hello</content></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>bash</name><command>cargo test</command></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>search_files</name><query>TODO</query></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>list_directory</name><path>src/</path></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>web_search</name><query>rust async</query></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>fetch_url</name><url>https://example.com</url></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>task_complete</name><summary>Done</summary></tool_call>
<tool_call><name>spawn_agent</name><task>Fix tests</task></tool_call>
```

## HTTP Daemon API

Start: `vibecli serve --port 7878 --provider claude`

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /health | Health check |
| POST | /chat | Single-turn chat |
| POST | /chat/stream | Streaming chat (SSE) |
| POST | /agent | Start agent task |
| GET | /stream/:id | SSE agent events |
| GET | /jobs | List jobs |
| POST | /jobs/:id/cancel | Cancel job |

Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <VIBECLI_API_TOKEN>`

## MCP Server

```bash
vibecli --mcp-server
```

Claude Desktop config:
```json
{"mcpServers":{"vibecli":{"command":"vibecli","args":["--mcp-server"],"cwd":"/project"}}}
```

Tools exposed: read_file, write_file, list_directory, bash, search_files, agent_run.

## ACP Protocol (Agent Communication Protocol)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /acp/v1/capabilities | Agent capabilities |
| POST | /acp/v1/tasks | Submit task |
| GET | /acp/v1/tasks/:id | Task status |
| GET | /acp/v1/tasks/:id/events | SSE events |

## WASM Extensions (VibeUI)

Manifest (`extension.json`):
```json
{"name":"ext","version":"1.0.0","display_name":"Ext","author":"Dev","description":"...","permissions":["FileRead","Notify"],"wasm_path":"extension.wasm"}
```

Permissions: FileRead, FileWrite, Network, ProcessExec, EnvRead, Notify, Clipboard.

Host module: `vibeui_host`. Host functions: log, read_file, write_file, notify.
Extension exports: alloc, init, on_file_save, on_text_change.
Max memory: 64 MiB.

## Configuration (~/.vibecli/config.toml)

```toml
[claude]
api_key = "sk-..."
model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"

[openai]
api_key = "sk-..."
model = "gpt-4o"

[ollama]
model = "llama3.2"

[failover]
providers = ["claude", "openai", "ollama"]

[[hooks]]
event = "PostToolUse"
tools = ["write_file"]
handler = { command = "sh hooks/format.sh" }

[[mcp_servers]]
name = "github"
command = "npx"
args = ["@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
```

Env vars: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GROK_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, VIBECLI_API_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN.

## Build Commands

```bash
cargo build --release -p vibecli          # CLI binary
cd vibeui && npm install && npm run tauri dev  # Desktop app
cargo test --workspace                     # All tests
```

## Full Documentation

See docs/PLUGIN-DEVELOPMENT.md for the complete plugin development guide with examples, API references, and WASM extension tutorials.
